records.ety: [Icel. leyna; akin to G. läugnen to deny, AS. lgnian, also E. lie to speak falsely.]

[OE. lenen, AS. hlinian, hleonian, v. i.; akin to OS. hlinōn, D. leunen, OHG. hlinēn, linēn, G. lehnen, L. inclinare, Gr. kli`nein, L. clivus hill, slope. √40. Cf. Declivity, Climax, Incline, Ladder.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Leaned (lēnd), sometimes Leant (lnt); p. pr. & vb. n. Leaning.]

defs: To incline, deviate, or bend, from a vertical position; to be in a position thus inclining or deviating; as, she leaned out at the window; a leaning column.
To incline in opinion or desire; to conform in conduct; -- with to, toward, etc.
quotes: They delight rather to lean to their old customs.

defs: To rest or rely, for support, comfort, and the like; -- with on, upon, or against.
quotes: He leaned not on his fathers but himself.

records.ety: [From Lean, v. i.; AS. hlǣnan, v. t., fr. hleonian, hlinian, v. i.]

defs: To cause to lean; to incline; to support or rest.
quotes: His fainting limbs against an oak he leant.

records.ety: [OE. lene, AS. hlǣne; prob. akin to E. lean to incline. See Lean, v. i. ]

defs: Wanting flesh; destitute of or deficient in fat; slim; not plump; slender; meager; thin; lank; as, a lean body; a lean cattle.

Wanting fullness, richness, sufficiency, or productiveness; deficient in quality or contents; slender; scant; barren; bare; mean; -- used literally and figuratively; as, the lean harvest; a lean purse; a lean discourse; lean wages.
quotes: What the land is, whether it be fat or lean.
Out of my lean and low ability I'll lend you something.

defs: Of a character which prevents the compositor from earning the usual wages; -- opposed to fat; as, lean copy, matter, or type.
That part of flesh which consists principally of muscle without the fat.
quotes: The fat was so white and the lean was so ruddy.
